programmes.show_description: The WDR Symphony Orchestra and conductor Ingo Metzmacher perform Helen Grime's Near Midnight and Mahler's Symphony No 7. Jonathan Swain presents.
Mark Forrest presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Sarah Walker with three hours of classical music to reflect, restore and refresh, including Beethoven's complete Moonlight Sonata by the legendary Alfred Brendel.
Sir Ian Blatchford, director and chief executive of the Science Museum Group, shares his musical choices with Michael Berkeley.
Sara Mohr-Pietsch explores the vernal landscape that surrounds Delius' joyous ode to spring, including Beethoven's own 'Pastoral' and Takemitsu's Tokyo cherry blossom.
From St Michael's Church, Barnes, London, with the choir of Tiffin School and music by Oliver Tarney and Howells.
Alyn presents jazz records of all styles as requested by you, including music from saxophonists Melissa Aldanni & Chris Potter, vocalists Betty Bennett & Nancy Wilson & Frank Zappa.
Mark Seow explores the subject of echoes, from Ovid's Roman myth of Echo to the echo effects employed by Gabrieli at St Mark's in Venice and the science behind echoes themselves.
Poetry, prose and music celebrating the English country calendar to mark 250 years since the birth of the painter John Constable.
An evocative sonic journey into the lives of elephants through the work of expert Joyce Poole, drawing on her Elephant Ethogram - a remarkable online archive of elephant calls.
Sophie Oliver guides us inside the remarkable home of Gertrude Stein and Alice Toklas in Paris and focuses on the material objects they cherished as a window onto their world.
The Building a Library recommended recording from yesterday's Record Review.
Kate Molleson and Gillian Moore shine new light on the 'modern' music of the 20th century. This week, Kate explores the sonic realms conjured by Canadian composer Claude Vivier.
A reflective service of night prayer from the Church of St Mary the Virgin, Tetbury. With words and music for the end of the day sung by St Martin's Voices.
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Open your ears for sounds and selections that celebrate the great outdoors as Elizabeth Alker announces the winner of the Tune into Nature Music Prize.
Soloist Stefan Dohr joins the Swedish Radio Symphony Orchestra and conductor Daniel Harding in Jörg Widmann's Horn Concerto plus works by Dvorák and Stravinsky.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
A showcase of great performances from the UK and abroad, including Czech masterpieces and live music-making from Wigmore Hall from pianists Pavel Kolesnikov and Samson Tsoy.

programmes.show_name: Composer of the Week: Thomas Adès
programmes.show_description: 1/5. Donald Macleod talks to Thomas Adès about the music that first propelled him to stardom, while still in his twenties, the composer's first opera, Powder Her Face.
Pianist Siqian Li performs live in the studio, and Petroc talks to the Unicorn Theatre's Artistic Director, Rachel Bagshaw.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical music.
A concert of Strauss, Prokofiev and Elgar from the Sinfonia of London, conducted by John Wilson as part of its Glasshouse residency in Gateshead.
...Cities of the Imagination. Erland Cooper explores the mystery of Kitezh, Russia's 'invisible city', a spiritual refuge hidden beneath the waters of Lake Svetloyar.
Hannah Peel presents an adventurous, immersive soundtrack for late-night listening, from classical to contemporary and everything in between.
Jamie Cullum returns for a second time to sit in for Soweto this week on 'Round Midnight, sharing some of his favourite new releases and jazz classics.
